# HoaSen Table

HoaSen Table is a native SQL client for PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite. It focuses on grammar-aware SQL autocomplete, smart foreign-key JOIN snippets, relation inspection, virtualized million-row grids, and plugin-driven extensibility.

## Key URLs
- Home: https://hoasentable.com/
- Blog: https://hoasentable.com/blog/

## Articles

  Summary: Executive Summary This technical whitepaper provides an exhaustive, micro-architectural analysis of Hybrid Transactional/Analytical Processing (HTAP) database systems. Historically, the database industry accepted a hard bifurcation between OLTP (Online Transaction Processing) and OLAP (Online Analytical Processing). HTAP breaks this compromise. - The Micro-Architectural Execution Mechanics Behind SQL Window Functions: https://hoasentable.com/technical-whitepaper-the-micro-architectural-execution-mechanics-behind-sql-window-functions/
  Summary: Overview SQL window functions changed how data engineers write analytical queries. Unlike aggregate functions, which collapse a group of rows into a single value, window functions keep every input row intact while still computing a value derived from a surrounding &#8220;window frame&#8221; around it. The syntax looks deceptively simple — ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY... ORDER BY...) [&hellip;]

  Summary: Why This Architecture Matters The query optimizer is the part of a relational database that decides whether your query finishes in milliseconds or brings the server to its knees. There&#8217;s no clever indexing trick or bigger disk that fixes it — the physics of one disk and one CPU cap what you can do. [&hellip;]

  Summary: Introduction Every distributed database eventually runs into the same question: how do you keep replicas in sync without either saturating the network or pegging every CPU core on the box? - Vector Clocks and CRDTs: How Distributed Databases Resolve Conflicting Writes: https://hoasentable.com/vector-clocks-and-crdts-the-art-of-resolving-distributed-data-conflicts-an-in-depth-technical-report/
  Summary: Why Availability Forces You to Deal With Conflicts When Amazon built the original Dynamo architecture, and later when Basho built Riak, both teams made the same trade: give up strong consistency to keep the system available even when parts of the network can&#8217;t talk to each other. That&#8217;s the trade behind the &#8220;five nines&#8221; uptime [&hellip;]
